name: Apigee API Management
what_it_does: Google's enterprise API management platform providing API gateway, developer portal, analytics, monetization, and policy-based traffic management, managed as a Google Cloud service.
use_cases: Enterprise API gateway policy management (rate limiting, quota, security) via API
Developer portal and product catalog management for API monetization programs
API analytics and metrics retrieval for operational dashboards
Proxy deployment and configuration automation in CI/CD pipelines
OAuth2 token management and API key provisioning for developer access
not_for: Small teams or startups (Apigee is expensive enterprise software)
Teams not on Google Cloud or committed to GCP ecosystem
Simple API proxying without policy management needs
best_when: Your enterprise is on Google Cloud and needs robust API management with analytics, monetization, developer portal, and complex policy management at scale.
avoid_when: You're a small company, not on GCP, or don't need enterprise-grade API management features.

auth.notes: Google Cloud IAM-based authentication. Service accounts with roles/apigee.admin or granular roles. OAuth2 with Google identity provider. Follows standard Google Cloud auth patterns.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; evaluation environments available
pricing.paid_tiers: Apigee Pay-as-you-go from $0.0002/API call; Subscription plans from $500+/month
pricing.notes: Enterprise pricing. Among the most expensive API management solutions. Typically used by large enterprises with existing Google Cloud commitments.

performance.rate_limits: Depends on environment type and plan

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Google API style errors with code, message, and status fields. Operation-level errors for async operations include additional details. Generally follows Google Cloud error conventions.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Proxy and product creation is idempotent by name. Deployments use revision numbers for idempotent updates. Long-running operations tracked by operation ID.
known_agent_gotchas: Apigee has separate environments (dev, test, prod) that must be explicitly managed and deployed to
Proxy deployments are asynchronous — agents must poll for deployment success
Organization and environment hierarchy must be understood before making API calls
Apigee X (GCP-native) and Apigee hybrid have different management APIs
Policy XML must be well-formed — XML validation errors are not always descriptive
Rate limiting on the management API itself is strict — automate carefully
